claim: Web MCP enables websites to expose structured tools to AI agents instead of requiring agents to scrape HTML or take screenshots
actionable: Implement Web MCP on your website to expose search, form submission, and interaction capabilities as structured tools to agents

claim: Web MCP provides declarative (for forms) and imperative (for complex interactions) APIs; declarative works for ~80% of existing HTML forms without modification
actionable: Start with declarative API for existing HTML forms; implement imperative API for dynamic, JavaScript-heavy interactions

claim: Web MCP dramatically reduces tokens needed for agent web interaction; one structured tool call replaces dozens of screenshot-and-guess interactions
actionable: Measure token savings by comparing Web MCP tool calls to screenshot-based interaction; expect 10-100x reduction per action

claim: Web MCP three pillars: context (what agent needs to understand), capabilities (actions on user's behalf), coordination (human-in-loop when agent is uncertain)
actionable: Design Web MCP endpoints around these three pillars; provide context about page state, define available actions, and support coordination feedback

claim: Web MCP is currently behind a Chrome flag in version 146; expected to roll out widely within months at Google Cloud Next or Google I/O
actionable: Monitor Chrome release notes; prepare Web MCP implementations now so you're ready when broader adoption occurs
